# Budgeted review closure: deterministic RC gate, verdict ladder, terminal contracts

**Priority: HIGH — the pipeline's enforcement leaf.** The operator's 1–2-RC economic ceiling is breached daily (5/7 open PRs beyond it at yesterday's audit); the voluntary two-strike discipline (Grace/Emmy/Euclid, adopted 2026-07-16) holds the line INTERIM, so this is important-not-panicked: one careful coherent PR beats a fast one — the byte gate and its own ≤2-RC AC make this leaf eat its own cooking. Existing epic-lane commitments (e.g. the #15243 spike) stay first for their holders.

## Context

The H/I activation lane graduated from D#15256 (quorum closed 2026-07-16: fable `AUTHOR_SIGNAL` + GPT `GRADUATION_APPROVED` at `DC_kwDODSospM4BDYFt`; Gemini `operator_benched` with the revalidation AC below). Operator economics: the 1–2-RC ceiling; the day's measured evidence: PR `#15208` at 79,203 bytes / 7 formal reviews to merge, five of seven open PRs beyond the ceiling, five D+S closures in one day. The circuit-breaker's own state-(b) "converging → full review" clause authorized the loop it was meant to cap.

## The Problem

The review pipeline's cost curve inverts after ~2 REQUEST_CHANGES cycles on ordinary surfaces while nothing mechanical stops the loop: RC is the only must-fix lever, per-cycle machinery is size-invariant, prose heuristics can't gate, and the `reviewDecision` enum lies about semantically-discharged PRs.

## The Architectural Reality

One coherent activation lane (Euclid's Step-Back §6: a per-surface split would reproduce the micro-PR cost this kills). The consumer map (graduated body, cycle-6): `.agents/skills/pr-review/SKILL.md` + guide + full/follow-up/micro templates + `review-cost-circuit-breaker.md` · `PullRequestService.managePrReview` + GraphQL projection + OpenAPI + service tests · `agent-pr-review-body-lint.yml` (stays post-submit bypass telemetry) · `ai/scripts/diagnostics/review-cost-meter.mjs` · `pull-request`'s review-response protocol + Maintainer Polish path · graph/retrospective consumers · human `reviewDecision` surfaces · the direct `gh pr review` fallback (disposition required). GitHub review verdicts are immutable — the mechanics ride `COMMENTED` + `acknowledgedRequestChanges`; the terminal D+S is itself a `CHANGES_REQUESTED` object the gate permits as the ONE validated exception.

## The Fix

One PR:

1. **The deterministic refusal gate in `manage_pr_review`:** every post-cutover submitted `CHANGES_REQUESTED` counts as one ordinary RC (honest retractions INCLUDED); at RC2 the budget is spent — the mutation refuses a third ordinary RC before GitHub creates it; the one exception is a validator-confirmed `Drop+Supersede` body carrying the Option-I completeness contract. Machinery correctives are prevented pre-submit via the existing shared validator. Cutover: PRs `createdAt` after activation (projection gains `createdAt`); older PRs grandfathered (ladder-as-judgment, never mechanical freeze). Any surviving override is a named auditable field.
2. **The guide replacement (net-smaller):** state (b) of §6.3's classifier DELETES by name; §9 becomes the four-row verdict ladder (nits inline/Maintainer-Polish · delivered-scope defects RC-budgeted with the RC2 closure partition · scope expansion via guarded A+FU transfer · D+S at any cycle for premise death, late terminal for no-merge-safe-slice) + the A+FU four-part guardrail (merge-safe head; no correctness deferral; close-target AC ownership explicit; the day-after-merge counterfactual test) + the D+S completeness contract (source-coordinate falsifiers · salvage map · disposition-shaped successor landing pad · successor cites the map) + the `disposition: implementation-off | ticket-prescription-off | ticket-premise-dead` enum. H's RC2 closure packet (consumer sweep · falsifier/property matrix · carried-vs-new census · truth-fold · semantic-surface freeze with the exactly-the-RA-named-capability rule and the property-refinement-within-frozen-surface carve-out).
3. **Fallback disposition:** the direct `gh pr review` path is closed, routed through the same preflight, or plainly documented as bypass-with-telemetry (the managed path fails closed).
4. **Instrumentation:** `review-cost-meter.mjs` gains the OQ5 metric set (ordinary-RC count · unique heads · carried-vs-new clusters · falsifier-classes-per-cycle + curve shape · findings-preventable-upstream · bytes · RC2→terminal time); fix the stale meter path in `review-cost-circuit-breaker.md`.

## Contract Ledger

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Behavior | Fallback / Edge Case | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `manage_pr_review` (mutation) | D#15256 OQ1 `[RESOLVED_TO_AC]` | refuses the 3rd ordinary post-cutover RC pre-creation; permits the validated terminal D+S exception; `createdAt` cutover; named override field | grandfathered PRs untouched; `COMMENTED`/`APPROVED` never gated | OpenAPI description + guide | focused service tests incl. the refusal, the exception, the cutover boundary, and the override audit |
| `pr-review` guide §6.3/§9 (+ templates) | the graduated ladder/guardrail/contracts | state-(b) deleted; the new verdict machine as §9 | micro-delta template survives for post-RC2 `COMMENTED` closure | the guide itself | **merge-eligibility gate: combined `review-cost-circuit-breaker.md` + `pr-review-guide.md` < 41,357 B** (measured baseline 4,506 + 36,851) |
| `review-cost-meter.mjs` | OQ5 metric set | emits the stratified metrics; same-head pairs content-classified in the METRICS layer only | raw counts remain available | tool `--help` | meter run on the `#15208`/`#15226`/`#15238`/`#15229` corpus reproduces the graduated numbers |

## Discussion Criteria Mapping

D#15256 `[RESOLVED_TO_AC]`s → this leaf: OQ1 (the deterministic gate, items 1/3 of the Fix) · OQ2 (Maintainer-Polish broadened trigger, in §9's ladder row) · OQ3 (no unchecked must-fix rides APPROVED — encoded in the A+FU guardrail) · OQ4 (the freeze + carve-out, in the closure packet) · OQ6 (the byte gate, in the ledger row + AC) · OQ5 (the meter, Fix item 4). Options A/B/D/E/F compose INTO this lane per the graduated body; C survives as the Maintainer-Polish branch; I's completeness contract + disposition enum are §9 text.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The refusal gate behaves per the ledger row (tests: refusal at RC3, the validated terminal exception, cutover boundary, grandfathering, the audit override).
- [ ] State (b) is deleted BY NAME; the four-row ladder + guardrail + completeness contract + disposition enum are the live §9; the RC2 closure-packet contract is in the guide.
- [ ] **Merge-eligibility byte gate: the combined two-file boundary is < 41,357 B after the replacement** (the PR body carries the measured before/after).
- [ ] The `gh pr review` fallback disposition is implemented and documented (closed / routed / bypass-with-telemetry stated).
- [ ] The meter emits the OQ5 set; the circuit-breaker file's stale tool path is fixed (or the file is absorbed/deleted by the replacement).
- [ ] **Post-merge (binding, from the graduated falsifier): the next five post-activation, non-grandfathered qualifying PRs are sampled, STRATIFIED (enforcement/security-adjacent vs ordinary product/metadata); failure per the graduated thresholds reopens D#15256** per its `revalidationTrigger`.
- [ ] **On Gemini reactivation:** `npm run ai:revalidation-sweep -- --family gemini --apply`; retroactive signal invited; `APPROVED`/`ABSTAIN` resolves, `DEFERRED` reopens peer reconciliation.
- [ ] Cross-family review (≤2 ordinary RC cycles — this leaf eats its own cooking).

## Out of Scope

Option G (the sibling intake leaf) · retrofitting the gate onto grandfathered PRs · any runtime/engine surface.
